exampleSentences: Hèn chi hôm nay anh ấy vui như vậy, anh ấy vừa được thăng chức.
Chị bị cảm, hèn chi chị không đi làm hôm nay.
Trời mưa to quá, hèn chi đường bị kẹt xe.
notes_md:
**hèn chi** = “no wonder” / “that explains why.” You use it when you suddenly understand the reason for something.

It’s a reaction phrase, usually at the beginning of a sentence when you realize the cause. It links a situation you just noticed to a reason that makes sense.


Very close to **thảo nào** (slightly more common and neutral). Think: you get new info → everything clicks → *hèn chi*.
notes:
hèn chi = “no wonder” / “that explains why.” You use it when you suddenly understand the reason for something.

It’s a reaction phrase, usually at the beginning of a sentence when you realize the cause. It links a situation you just noticed to a reason that makes sense.

Very close to thảo nào (slightly more common and neutral). Think: you get new info → everything clicks → hèn chi.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He is so happy today, no wonder he just got promoted.
You have a cold, no wonder you didn't go to work today.
It's raining heavily, no wonder the street is jammed.
